content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines. The first line contains a positive integer N, the number of integers in the given array. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN containing the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the maximal XOR of 3 chosen numbers in the array.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the maximum XOR of 3 chosen numbers is 3 (1^2^3 = 0, but 1^2^2 = 3). For the second test case, the maximum XOR of 3 chosen numbers is 3 (2^2^3 = 3).
The maximum XOR of 3 chosen numbers is 7 (1^2^4 = 7).
The maximum XOR of 3 chosen numbers is 0 (1^1^1 = 0).

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by iterating over all possible combinations of three numbers in the array and calculating their XOR. The maximum XOR value is updated whenever a larger XOR value is found.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^3) per test case, O(T * N^3) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space complexity is constant as we only use a fixed amount of space to store the input and the maximum XOR value.
